entry.title: SAP SuccessFactors – Scrambling Framework via Integration Center
entry.content:
Business Case:

Instance refresh(snapshot) is a common routine in any industry to perform from Production instance to Lower instances (Dev, Test, Training, so on). This is applicable for most of the applications in the software world. The below topic provides insights on automating the anonymization process involved in SAP SuccessFactors to lower instance, by default there is very little offering from SAP on the anonymizing of data to lower instance. 

Why instance refresh is being done?

  1. To ensure that Production config is in sync with other instances and ensures the new features/configuration is done on close to prod configuration and tested before moving it to production.
  2. Easy to troubleshoot, as the prod issues can be replicated in lower instances, find the root cause and try the possible solution to fix the issue.

Process statement:

As all the production data is copied over to lower instance, it is important to anonymous the sensitive data in the lower instance before it is released for testers, country admin users and target systems. There is no or very little automation possibility available from SuccessFactors to anonymous the data from SAP SuccessFactors for very few fields. 

Here are the process steps involved.

  • Export the data

  • Extract the reports

  • Export the csv template for each portlet

  • Excel formulas to scramble identified sensitive info.

  • Update each template with the scrambled info

  • Import the updated template back into SF instance

  • Post Refresh activities

And scrambling(anonymous) the data is very time consuming process especially incase the volume of records is more in the system. Until the lower instance is anoymized, the system will be locked.

Solution:

To mitigate the above problem, Integration Center came into rescue to automate the process of anonymization and also saved lot of manual efforts spent.

The Integration jobs for each portlet needs to created (one time activity) in Integration Center which requires anonymization. This eliminates below manual steps

  1. Export the data
  2. Extract the reports
  3. Export the csv template for each portlet
  4. Update each template with the scrambled info
  5. Import the updated template back into SF instance

Here is the picture from demo system on how the jobs are created. I have intentionally not posted the scripts as it could vary from customer to customer. Creating Integration center jobs are straight forward and built on top of OData API, familiarize with OData dictionary, there are lot of blogs on the same.

Once the jobs are run successfully, data validation can be performed by exporting the data from the system. The above is a case study which saved weeks of effort for our team.

Hope this blog helps to automate your anonymization process. Thank you for reading!

@RajasekarVenkatachalaiah - Rajasekar Venkatachalaiah

entry.title: The Latest in SAP Best Practices for SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central 1H 2024
entry.content:
It's that time of the year when we release enhancements to our SAP Best Practices content for SAP SuccessFactors HCM. With the latest 1H 2024 release, we are excited to announce that we have made a lot of updates to the standard configuration content, documentation, and sample employee data. Here are the key highlights in the SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central area (without Time Management).

Reimagined Enrollment for US Benefits

The 1H 2024 release brings a reimagined enrollment experience for US Benefits. This new interface focuses on enhancing user interaction and simplifying the enrollment process. In its first phase, the update covers Insurance and Savings Plans, providing a more intuitive and efficient way for employees to manage their benefits. This enhancement is part of SAP's ongoing efforts to improve user experience and streamline benefits administration. The Best Practices content now incorporates this new enrollment experience out of the box.

Here is a snapshot of the new UI.

New validation rules for Dependents Management

The following validations are added:

  • For Malaysia, a validation rule to validate the passport number of the dependents
  • For Singapore, a validation rule for the field Confinement Number - the rule should allow a number from 1 to 99 else it should throw an error message
  • For Thailand, a validation rule for the fields - Spouse's Father's ID Number and Spouse's Mother's ID Number
  • For the USA, a validation rule for the field "Non Taxable" - field is allowed only for 'Domestic Partner' and 'Child of Domestic' relationships, while for others, it should throw an error message

Rule Scenario Updates

We have been updating the application-based rule scenarios since the last few releases. In 1H 2024, we migrated most of the business rules in Employee Central Core country localizations to the relevant rule scenarios. These changes include migrating the business rules from "basic" to "onChangeEvent" and "newHireScenario" scenarios in most cases. However, the "basic" scenario still applies to some business rules - these will be migrated in the upcoming releases.

Simplification of EC Core Alerts

As per the recommendation, the alert business rules are simplified now. The alerts sent one month and one week before the probation end date are triggered by two different business rules, respectively. A similar strategy is adopted for alert rules related to contract end date. Here are the details:

  • SFCC_Alert_Probation_Reminder is split into SFCC_Alert_Probation_Reminder_1Month and SFCC_Alert_Probation_Reminder_7Days
  • SFCC_AlertForContractEnd is split into SFCC_AlertForContractEnd_1Month and SFCC_AlertForContractEnd_7Days

New Event Reason for Pensioners

Per the product recommendation, a new Event Reason, TERIDEATP (Death of Pensioner), is added to the SAP Best Practices content. This event reason will work with an already-existing Event Reason TERECPP (Retirement with company pension) for a pensioner. This addition will allow customers to capture the correct data for their pensioners and help with accurate reporting.

The Supervisor field is mandatory in Job Information

As per the feedback we got recently, the following changes have been made to the Supervisor field:

  • It is made mandatory
  • If the customer deploys best practices content for Position Management, this field is read-only.

New Workflow for Work Order Extensions

A new workflow has been introduced to extend work orders for contingent workers. This workflow, initiated by the supervisor, streamlines the process of managing extensions, making it easier to handle contract renewals. This update is particularly beneficial for organizations with a large contingent workforce, as it automates and simplifies the extension process, reducing administrative overhead and improving operational efficiency.

Changes to Net Gains in India Tax Declaration

Significant changes have been made to processing net gains for the India Income Tax Declaration. The changes in the configuration are in the Investment Types and Declaration Types. These updates aim to ensure compliance with local tax regulations while simplifying the declaration process for users. By refining the handling of net gains, SAP SuccessFactors helps users navigate the complexities of tax filing with greater ease and accuracy, ensuring that all necessary information is captured and processed correctly.

Renaming of Permission Roles

In alignment with the SAP SuccessFactors Process Library, the names of Permission Roles in Employee Central Core, Company Structure Overview, and Position Management have been updated. This renaming effort enhances clarity and consistency across the platform, making it easier for users to understand and manage permission roles. 

New Workflow for Reporting a No-Show

A new workflow has been added to address situations where employees fail to show up on their first day. This workflow is automatically triggered in such cases, allowing HR teams to respond and take necessary actions quickly. This addition helps organizations manage no-show scenarios more effectively, ensuring minimized disruptions and appropriate follow-up actions are taken promptly.

New Business Rule for Future Birth Dates

A new business rule has been implemented to check if an entered birth date is in the future during employee data changes. This rule prevents erroneous data entry, ensuring only valid birth dates are recorded. By enforcing this validation, SAP SuccessFactors helps maintain data integrity and accuracy, reducing the likelihood of errors in employee records.

Updates to Sample Employee Data

The sample employee data has been updated to include new localizations for Time Off in Finland and Ireland. These additions expand the regional coverage of sample data, making it more relevant and valuable for organizations operating in these countries. The updated sample data helps organizations better understand and configure the Time Off module to meet local requirements.

In addition, as per the feedback we got recently, the field "second manager" has been removed from all the sample employees.

SAP Help Portal

We have been improving the structure of the Best Practices content for SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central on the SAP Help Portal. The changes include ease of navigation and quick access to documentation. A user can review the complete Best Practices content for one or more countries using the country dropdown, as shown in the screenshot below.

Finally, with every release, we move forward on our mission to provide maximum value to our customers with the best practices content. Please reach out to me in case you have any questions or comments.

entry.title: Enabling Job Family/ Job Sub Family in Position Management and Employee Profile.
entry.content:
Hi Folks,

In today's time a very common ask from the clients is to enable the Job Family and Job Sub Family fields in Position Management and Employee Profile UI.

Job Function <Job Family> designed in the Job classification object and many organizations also have custom Job Sub Function <Job Sub Family> associated with Job Code.

The following steps will guide in setting up Job Function <Job Family> fields visibility in Position and propagating the values directly after selection of Job code. Synching the field values from Position to Job Info: 

  • Go to Configure Object Definition- Take action in Position object

    Enter Job Family and Job Subfamily details and save

  • Now to go to Position setup from Manage Configuration UI- click Add Field to make it visible in UI:
  •   
  •  

 

         

 

 

  • Apply the condition in business rule to propagate the Job Family/ Sub Family value by selecting the Job Code in Position: 
  • To make fields available in Job Info portlet got to - Manage Business configuration:

  • In search tool enter position management setting and select Synchronization Tab:

         Open the existing sync rule and add:

  • The visibility permission for these fields in Employee Profile- Job Info Portlet can be enabled for respective user group as per business ask from Manage Permission Role:

This solution steps will deliver client requirement.

Regards,

Papia

entry.title: Speeding up your SAP HCM move to the cloud in 2024 - Part 3: SAP HCM ROI Calculator
entry.content:
Are you considering making the move from on-prem HCM to SAP SuccessFactors but struggling to quantify the potential return on investment (ROI)? Look no further, because SAP has just the tool for you - the SAP HCM ROI Calculator.

HCM ROI Calculation example

 

This free, user-friendly calculator is based on the study "Analyzing the Economic Benefits of Moving from SAP ERP HCM to SAP SuccessFactors," conducted by Enterprise Strategy Group (ESG). The study found that organizations can realize a typical 107% return on investment when making the switch. The calculator allows users to enter as few as 4 data points to get started, providing a quick and easy way to estimate the potential ROI of moving to SAP SuccessFactors.  You can also access the complete ESG study directly from the same page, as well as sign up for a free HCM Customer Evolution Kit workshop, in which SAP experts can help you to formulate a winning plan for your move to the cloud.

But that's not all - for those who want to dive deeper into the analysis, the calculator offers more than 20 optional additional data points that allow users to fine-tune their model and get a more customized ROI estimation and further explore the potential financial benefits of making the transition to SAP SuccessFactors.

The calculator is available now and is a valuable tool for organizations looking to make an informed decision about their HCM system. Whether you're in the early stages of considering a move or are ready to take the next steps, the SAP HCM ROI Calculator can provide you with the insights you need to make a well-informed decision.

We also recognize that for some customers, the SAP HCM ROI Calculator will be more of a conversation starter, or a source of input for a more extensive business case.  If this is your situation, then our SAP Value Advisors can help.  Should this be of interest, please contact your SAP account representative to get started.

In addition to the potential financial benefits, SAP SuccessFactors offers a range of features and capabilities that can help businesses keep up with the evolving demands of the modern workforce. From talent management and employee engagement tools to analytics and reporting capabilities, SAP SuccessFactors provides a comprehensive HCM solution that can drive business success.

This post completes a 3-part series intended to help speed your HCM move to the cloud in 2024. 

In Part 1 of this blog series, we reviewed the SAP Readiness Check for SAP SuccessFactors solutions

In Part 2 of this blog series, we showcased the SAP HCM Evolution Kit workshop

In conclusion, the SAP HCM ROI Calculator is a game-changer for organizations considering a move to SAP SuccessFactors. With a typical 107% return on investment and a user-friendly interface, it's a valuable tool for anyone looking to understand the potential financial benefits of making the switch. Don't miss out on this opportunity to gain insights into the economic benefits of moving from SAP ERP HCM to SAP SuccessFactors – please try the calculator today and see how it can help your organization!
